privacy.notes: Metadata-only JVM binding contract. It describes Kotlin-first request/response models without embedding calculator kernels, proprietary tables, or patient-level data.

calculator_contract_alignment.compatibility_policy: Keep request and response model names stable across Kotlin and Java consumers, and fail closed when the public calculator contract version or required column set changes.

request_response_models.label: Calculator request model
request_response_models.notes: Represents a typed Kotlin-first request for a public calculator run or binding invocation.

request_response_models.label: Calculator response model
request_response_models.notes: Represents a typed Kotlin-first response that can carry service or fallback results with diagnostics.

request_response_models.label: Binding diagnostic model
request_response_models.notes: Captures transport choice, schema drift, fixture-gate results, and other adapter-visible diagnostics.

request_response_models.label: Binding error model
request_response_models.notes: Stable error envelope for Kotlin and Java callers when validation, transport, or fixture gates fail.

surfaces.label: Service binding
surfaces.notes: Primary request/response path for calculator execution through the public service surface.

surfaces.label: Arrow fallback surface
surfaces.notes: Deterministic fallback for cross-language bulk interchange and JVM-friendly table exchange.

surfaces.label: Parquet fallback surface
surfaces.notes: Persisted fallback for offline fixture replay, batch validation, and round-trip parity checks.

surfaces.label: Diagnostics surface
surfaces.notes: Machine-readable diagnostics for binding selection, contract drift, and fixture-gate outcomes.

surfaces.label: Error surface
surfaces.notes: Stable error envelope exposed to Kotlin and Java callers.

surfaces.label: Fixture gate surface
surfaces.notes: Declared gates that keep synthetic pass and fail examples reproducible.

transport_fallbacks.when: The public calculator service is available and the contract version matches the caller request.
transport_fallbacks.notes: Use the service path first when the runtime can satisfy the request directly.

transport_fallbacks.when: Bulk tabular exchange is needed or the service path is unavailable but a binary table exchange is still possible.
transport_fallbacks.notes: Use Arrow to preserve typed tabular exchange across Kotlin and Java consumers.

transport_fallbacks.when: The binding needs persisted offline interchange, reproducible replay, or file-backed fixture parity.
transport_fallbacks.notes: Use Parquet only as the deterministic file-backed fallback.

diagnostics.trigger: The Kotlin or Java model drops a public calculator contract field or renames a required boundary field.
diagnostics.output: validation diagnostics with schema drift details and the missing field list.

diagnostics.trigger: The binding chooses service, Arrow, or Parquet based on runtime availability or payload shape.
diagnostics.output: selection diagnostics that explain which transport was used and why.

diagnostics.trigger: A fixture gate is satisfied only partially or a synthetic example is missing a declared companion fixture.
diagnostics.output: fixture-gate diagnostics with pass, fail, or blocked status.

diagnostics.trigger: A request or response records provenance from a source contract or interchange file.
diagnostics.output: provenance diagnostics that preserve source path, checksum, and transport metadata.

errors.message: calculator request is missing required public-contract fields
errors.notes: Use this when the binding cannot map a Kotlin or Java request to the public calculator contract.

errors.message: unsupported calculator or pricing year for the JVM binding
errors.notes: Use this when the caller requests a calculator or pricing year outside the declared boundary.

errors.message: service unavailable and no declared fallback could be used
errors.notes: Use this when the service path fails and both Arrow and Parquet fallback paths are unavailable.

errors.message: fixture gate rejected the synthetic binding example
errors.notes: Use this when a pass or fail example violates the declared metadata-only fixture policy.

fixture_gates.description: Committed fixtures remain synthetic and metadata only.
fixture_gates.evidence: No calculator kernels, proprietary tables, or PHI are included in the bundle.

fixture_gates.description: The JVM binding models remain aligned to the public calculator contract fields.
fixture_gates.evidence: Request and response model fields include schema version, calculator_id, pricing_year, and column boundaries.

fixture_gates.description: Transport fallback order is explicit and deterministic.
fixture_gates.evidence: Service, Arrow, and Parquet are ordered by priority in the contract.

fixture_gates.description: Pass and fail examples are paired with stable diagnostics and error envelopes.
fixture_gates.evidence: The bundle includes dedicated pass, fail, diagnostics, and error examples.

fixture_gates.description: Maven and Gradle readiness is declared without committing generated binaries.
fixture_gates.evidence: The build-readiness section records Kotlin, Java interop, and JVM target expectations.

maven.notes: Ready for a Maven Java library module that can publish the JVM binding models and client stubs.

gradle.notes: Ready for a Gradle build that can host Kotlin and Java sources together.

jvm.notes: Target the modern JVM baseline that works cleanly with Kotlin and Java consumers.

examples.name: validation-pass
examples.description: Synthetic pass example showing aligned JVM request and response models, declared fallback order, and ready build metadata.

examples.name: validation-fail
examples.description: Synthetic fail example showing model drift, missing fixture evidence, and blocked build readiness.

examples.name: diagnostics
examples.description: Synthetic diagnostics example showing transport selection and provenance capture.

examples.name: errors
examples.description: Synthetic stable error example for Kotlin and Java consumers.
